name: Sitchin Theory
description: The Sitchin Theory, popularized by Zecharia Sitchin in his "Earth Chronicles" series, posits that the ancient Sumerian texts describe extraterrestrial beings called the Anunnaki who came to Earth from a hypothetical planet called Nibiru. According to Sitchin, these Anunnaki genetically engineered humans as a slave race to mine gold. He interprets Sumerian cuneiform tablets and mythological accounts as literal historical records of this interaction. Sitchin's work gained significant traction in the late 20th century, influencing alternative history and ufology circles. His claims include the Anunnaki's arrival approximately 450,000 years ago, their advanced technology, and their role in shaping human civilization. Mainstream scholars, including Assyriologists and archaeologists, strongly reject Sitchin's interpretations. They argue that his translations of Sumerian texts are inaccurate, selective, and often based on misinterpretations of ancient symbols and concepts. They point out that the Sumerian texts are mythological and allegorical, not literal historical accounts of extraterrestrial intervention. Furthermore, the scientific community dismisses the existence of Nibiru, citing a lack of astronomical evidence. Sitchin's theory remains a controversial topic, fueling debate between proponents who see it as a groundbreaking revelation and skeptics who consider it pseudo-science.
subtitle: Anunnaki, Nibiru, and ancient astronauts.

claims.claim: The Anunnaki from Nibiru visited Earth.
claims.evidence: Sitchin's interpretations of Sumerian cuneiform tablets and mythological texts.
claims.criticism: Mainstream Assyriologists and archaeologists reject Sitchin's translations as inaccurate and selectively interpreted.

claims.claim: Humans were genetically engineered by the Anunnaki as a slave race.
claims.evidence: Sitchin's interpretation of Sumerian creation myths, suggesting a deliberate intervention in human evolution.
claims.criticism: Evolutionary biology and genetics provide a different explanation for human origins, with no evidence of extraterrestrial genetic manipulation.

claims.claim: Nibiru is a real planet with a highly elliptical orbit that brings it close to Earth every 3,600 years.
claims.evidence: Sitchin's interpretation of Sumerian astronomical knowledge.
claims.criticism: Astronomers have found no evidence of such a planet. Its supposed orbit would be highly unstable and disruptive to the solar system.

keyFigures.name: Zecharia Sitchin
keyFigures.role: Author and proponent of the Sitchin Theory

keyFigures.name: Michael S. Heiser
keyFigures.role: Biblical scholar and critic of Sitchin's interpretations

timeline.event: Publication of Sitchin's "The 12th Planet", introducing the Sitchin Theory to a wider audience.
Rise in popularity of the Sitchin Theory within alternative history and conspiracy theory circles.
Continued debate and criticism of the Sitchin Theory by mainstream scholars.

sources.author: Michael S. Heiser
sources.work: The Myth of a Fallen Hybrid Race
sources.title: Michael S. Heiser

sources.author: William Irwin Thompson
sources.work: The Time Falling Bodies Take to Light: Mythology, Sexuality, and the Origins of Culture
sources.title: William Irwin Thompson
